We are given that \( S(t) \) follows geometric Brownian motion, so  
\[
S(1) = S(0) e^{(0.05 - 0.5 \times 0.2^2) + 0.2 Z(1)} = 0.5 e^{0.03 + 0.2 Z(1)}.
\]  
Then  
\[
E[S(1)^\alpha] = 0.5^\alpha e^{\alpha \times 0.03 + 0.5 \alpha^2 \times 0.2^2} = 1.4.
\]  
Under risk-neutral pricing, the drift changes to \( r = 0.03 \), so the time-0 price is  
\[
